purpose: Define how voiceover, subtitles, and visual scenes are aligned after final TTS audio is generated.
workflow: Generate voiceover_units in video-script-maker. Each unit must be split into short sentence_units or semantic_units, not only paragraph-level narration.
Generate final TTS audio from voiceover_units.
Read the real TTS audio duration before rendering.
Run forced alignment or transcription timestamps when available. If unavailable, create a duration-fit preview timeline from the real audio duration and mark it as estimated_after_audio_duration_fit.
Write subtitle_timeline.generated.{orientation}.json from real audio timing or duration-fit timing.
Map each subtitle cue to voice_id and visual_section_id.
Render video-app from the generated subtitle timeline, not hand-estimated script timing.
For final publishable renders, replace duration-fit timing with forced_alignment or transcription_timestamps.
hard_rules: Final voiceover videos must not read subtitle cues directly from hand-written script estimates.
Subtitle cues must be short: normally 1 spoken sentence or 1 semantic clause per cue.
Each cue should target 1.5 to 4.5 seconds. Avoid 8+ second subtitle blocks unless the line is intentionally held.
Scene switching should follow visual_section_id boundaries derived from voiceover_units whenever practical.
If the voiceover audio duration differs from the planned video duration by more than 1 second, update the subtitle timeline and either retime scenes or add an intentional closing hold.

fallback_policy.when_forced_alignment_unavailable: Distribute cue durations from real audio duration across short sentence_units and mark timing_status as estimated_after_audio_duration_fit.
